ATPIHL1.title: ATPIHL1
ATPIHL1.memory: 2004. This is where it all began, where I first arrived in Vietnam. Right in the heart of the backpacker’s area. Almost impossible to be more detached from the so called “real Vietnam.

ATPIHL2.title: ATPIHL2
ATPIHL2.memory: Still 2004. The first apartment in Saigon. Block D. Finally leaving the stuffy hotel room in Bui Vien. 12th floor. We need to buy tickets to use the elevator. From the bedroom I can see the Zoo and the elephants.

ATPIHL3.title: ATPIHL3
ATPIHL3.memory: Return to Vietnam. Broken hearts need to heal. Villa Mille Occhi, Quang Trung Software City, Corporate Job. 2007. Dreams Lost, dreams found. Everything is possible this time around.

ATPIHL4.title: ATPIHL4
ATPIHL4.memory: Early 2008. I got to reinvent myself in this city where at this time the options seem limitless. It is the right time and the right place. Rented a room in D1 for 100 bucks a night without windows or aircon. My own founding myth.

ATPIHL5.title: ATPIHL5
ATPIHL5.memory: This must have been 2008. Photographing nightlife for a living. My place is in a quiet back alley in District 3. Saigon’s heartland. Next to a street called Vuon Chuoi, Banana Garden, I do like the sound of it.

ATPIHL6.title: ATPIHL6
ATPIHL6.memory: Got a better job with better pay. I decided to move to an old apartment building. Oh so cool, like in a Wong Kar Wai movie. But very soon it was clear that I did not belong.

ATPIHL7.title: ATPIHL7
ATPIHL7.memory: So I fled to suburbia. Saigon South. Things were easy there. We were living in a corporation.

ATPIHL8.title: ATPIHL8
ATPIHL8.memory: We must have stayed here for more than 10 years. This was truly my first home in Saigon. Safe haven during the pandemic. The lost years.

ATPIHL9.title: ATPIHL9
ATPIHL9.memory: All things change. Live throws you a curve ball. And then one more. We had to move again. I did not want to live in this new neigbourhood at first. It is going to be ok. I am still new here
